purpose: Source catalog for future Computer Vision dataset acquisition. The catalog does not download or commit copyrighted images; it documents candidate sources, allowed use checks, target classes and curation rules.
safetyBoundary: The visual model will return a visual similarity signal only. It cannot prove pedigree, breed purity, official registration or health status from a photo.

candidateSources.name: Tsinghua Dogs Dataset
candidateSources.knownContent: 130 dog breeds, 70,428 real-world images, one dog per image, whole-body and head bounding-box annotations described by the paper.
candidateSources.licenseAction: Review the dataset access terms before download and keep raw images out of the Git repository.
candidateSources.repoPolicy: Do not commit raw images. Commit only labels, manifests, source notes and small synthetic/demo placeholders when allowed.

candidateSources.name: Stanford Dogs Dataset
candidateSources.knownContent: 120 dog breeds and 20,580 images; TensorFlow Datasets describes 12,000 train and 8,580 test images with class labels and bounding boxes for training images.
candidateSources.licenseAction: Review original Stanford/ImageNet usage terms. Use as research/educational baseline only if permitted.
candidateSources.repoPolicy: Do not commit raw images. Use external data path or local ignored folder.

candidateSources.name: Kaggle Dog Breeds Image Dataset
candidateSources.knownContent: The data card describes 17,000+ dog breed images curated from Dog CEO API and organized by breed folders.
candidateSources.licenseAction: Review and verify Kaggle dataset license and original Dog CEO API usage notes before use.
candidateSources.repoPolicy: Do not commit raw downloaded dataset. Keep only a manifest of selected files and labels.

candidateSources.name: Owned / consented Cane Corso photos
candidateSources.knownContent: Photos taken by the owner or submitted with permission, labeled using the project photo guide.
candidateSources.licenseAction: Use only photos with explicit permission for project/training/demo use.
candidateSources.repoPolicy: Prefer keeping original photos outside Git. Commit only anonymized labels and derived metadata unless permission allows demo assets.

sourceReviewChecklist: Confirm image license and permitted usage before download.
Record source_id and source_url in the manifest.
Separate train/validation/test by image identity and, when possible, by dog identity to avoid leakage.
Filter duplicates and near-duplicates before training.
Reject AI-generated, watermarked, heavily edited or unclear images for training unless explicitly labeled as not_suitable_photo.
Label view_type, photo_quality, comparison_readiness and quality_issues before model training.
Keep raw images out of Git; use local data folders or external storage.
